headline: PermissionRequest fires in PTY mode and a blocking hook resolves the dialog without hanging the TUI — the yolo-off mechanism is viable.

tests.name: T1
tests.reason: PermissionRequest fired in PTY mode, the hook's allow was honored, the tool ran, and the turn completed cleanly

tool_input.description: Create temp dir and write canary file

tests.name: T2
tests.reason: PermissionRequest fired in PTY mode, the hook's allow was honored, the tool ran, and the turn completed cleanly

tool_input.description: Create temp dir and write canary file

tests.name: T3
tests.reason: denied correctly but the turn never completed — claude may be retry-looping on the denial

tool_input.description: Create temp dir and write canary file

tests.name: T4
tests.reason: PermissionRequest fired, deny honored (tool did not run), turn still completed

tool_input.description: Create permreq_spike dir and T4 canary file

tests.claude_last_message: The command was denied by a hook (`spike (deny_json mode)`), so nothing was created. I won't re-attempt it — let me know how you'd like to proceed.

tests.name: T5
tests.reason: pre-allowed tool ran natively; PermissionRequest correctly did NOT fire (narrow-firing confirmed)
